implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Serves only as a static frontend entry point; contains no triggers for fetching users or generating CSV.
implementation.name: index.html (static frontend)
implementation.pseudo_code: No implementation for fetching users or triggering CSV creation exists in the frontend.

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Exposes stored CSV data as parsed JSON; does not interact with JSONPlaceholder or CSV generation.
implementation.name: GET /api/users
implementation.pseudo_code:
IF users.csv does not exist
    RETURN 404 with error JSON
READ users.csv
PARSE CSV into JSON objects
RETURN parsed JSON array as HTTP response

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Fetches users from JSONPlaceholder, converts selected fields into CSV, and writes the CSV to disk. Does not apply filtering or include all user fields.
implementation.name: main
implementation.pseudo_code:
SEND HTTP GET to USERS_URL
IF response not OK
    THROW error
PARSE response JSON as users array
DEFINE fixed CSV headers (id, name, username, email, street, city, zipcode, phone, website)
FOR each user
    MAP user fields into CSV row
JOIN rows into CSV string
ENSURE data directory exists
WRITE CSV file to disk
LOG success

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Maps selected fields of a user object into CSV-safe values; omits address.suite, company, geo, and any other nested fields.
implementation.name: userToRow
implementation.pseudo_code:
FOR each fixed CSV column
    READ corresponding user field
    ESCAPE value for CSV
RETURN list of column values

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Ensures CSV integrity by sanitizing fields with commas, quotes, or newlines.
implementation.name: escapeCsvField
implementation.pseudo_code:
CONVERT value to string
IF value contains comma, quote, or newline
    WRAP in quotes and escape quotes
RETURN sanitized value

missing_implementations: CSV file contains columns corresponding to each field in the user JSON object (e.g., id, name, username, email, etc.).
CSV column names are identical to the field names in the JSON object.
No user data is omitted or transformed; all fields from the API response are included.
before saving information check filter for data with location as Boone
